source_pr.title: br: fix unstable test
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/43170

Problem Summary: fix unstable test

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Test assumes a stable ordering of `partitionMap` entries, but the order can vary between runs.
root_cause_explanation: The unit test was flaky because it assumed a deterministic order for `partitionMap` (e.g., always expecting `partitionMap[0]` to correspond to a specific upstream partition ID). In practice, the partition list can be produced in a non-deterministic order (often due to map iteration or non-stable aggregation). This PR fixes the flakiness by making the assertions order-insensitive: it checks which upstream ID appears first and validates the corresponding downstream mapping accordingly.
fix_pattern: Do not rely on the iteration order of maps/slices derived from maps in tests; sort by a stable key (e.g., ID) or make assertions order-insensitive (treat as a set).
